note_data.title: Cell structure and organisation
keyPoints: A **cell** is the basic unit of life that can exist as a single-celled organism or as part of a multicellular organism
**Cell membrane** is a partially permeable barrier that controls the movement of substances into and out of the cell
**Nucleus** contains chromosomes made of DNA which control the activities of the cell
**Cytoplasm** is a jelly-like substance where chemical reactions take place
**Mitochondria** are organelles where aerobic respiration occurs to release energy
**Chloroplasts** contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis in plant cells
A **tissue** is a group of similar cells working together to perform the same function
An **organ** is made up of different tissues working together to perform a particular function
**Magnification** = size of image ÷ actual size of object
Plant cells have a **cell wall** made of cellulose, a **permanent vacuole** filled with cell sap, and **chloroplasts**

definitions.definition: A group of similar cells working together to perform the same function
A group of different tissues working together to perform a particular function
A group of organs working together to perform a particular function
A structure inside a cell that has a specific function

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give similarities AND differences between two things
Show working clearly and include units in your answer

modelAnswer.question: State three differences between plant and animal cells. [3]
modelAnswer.answer: Plant cells have a cell wall made of cellulose, animal cells do not. Plant cells have chloroplasts, animal cells do not. Plant cells have a permanent vacuole, animal cells have small temporary vacuoles.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Cell wall present in plants only
Mark 2: Chloroplasts present in plants only
Mark 3: Permanent vacuole in plants vs small/temporary vacuoles in animals

commonMistakes: Never say cell wall is made of 'cellulose fiber' — always say 'cellulose'
Students often confuse cell membrane with cell wall — cell membrane is in ALL cells, cell wall only in plants
Don't say chloroplasts are 'green' — say they 'contain chlorophyll'
Never write 'times bigger' in magnification — use 'times' or 'x' only
Students forget to show working in magnification calculations
examinerTips: This topic appears in every paper — especially plant vs animal cell differences
Always draw labelled diagrams with straight lines and no arrowheads for labels
Magnification calculations are common — remember to show all working
Learn the hierarchy: cell → tissue → organ → organ system → organism
formulas: Magnification = size of image ÷ actual size of object
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Plant and animal cell diagrams showing nucleus, cytoplasm, cell membrane, mitochondria. Plant cell additionally shows cell wall, chloroplasts, and permanent vacuole. All parts must be clearly labelled.
